meta.title: Insurance Policy Administration
meta.catalog: Guidewire PolicyCenter — Policy Lifecycle (System-of-Record) Best-Practice Catalog

steps.output: Received via portal, agent or paper; application ID assigned; timestamp in PAS
Personal details confirmed; identity documents verified; discrepancies flagged
Coverage type, sum insured and period reviewed against product guidelines

steps.activity: Conduct risk assessment based on verified data
steps.output: Risk checklist (claims history, occupation, health, credit, driving); risk score assigned
Premium calculated on risk, coverage and rating factors; currency confirmed
Policy drafted with coverage, risk conditions, premium and terms; Legal input for non-standard clauses

steps.activity: Review and update policy terms and conditions
steps.output: Wording reviewed for accuracy, completeness and regulatory compliance; final terms confirmed

steps.activity: Notify policyholder of policy terms and price
steps.output: Formal notification (email/post): coverage, premium, schedule, start date, 10-day deadline

steps.activity: Obtain policyholder consensus on terms and price
steps.output: Acceptance via portal, signed form or recorded verbal; queries escalated
Payment via card, banking, cheque or transfer; coverage attaches only on confirmed payment
Final policy generated in PAS; policy number and effective date assigned; document linked
Schedule, certificate and PDS by email within 2 days; hard copy if required
PAS record updated and quality-checked within 1 business day of issuance
Automated 90/60/30-day pre-expiry alerts to policyholder and agent
Due dates tracked; reminders 7 days before due; missed payments flagged to Collections
Claim registered in CMS; claim ID issued; routed to SOP-INS-011 or SOP-INS-012

branches: Within authority → settle per claims SOP

steps.activity: Close policy at end of term or on request

swim_phases.name: Application & risk assessment
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–5 · receipt to pricing
swim_phases.note: Applications are verified for data, identity and coverage eligibility and pass an approval gate before risk assessment and pricing.

swim_phases.name: Drafting & acceptance
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 6–9 · draft to acceptance
swim_phases.note: Drafted policies pass a terms-approval gate before notification; disputes allow up to two revised offers before closing as not taken up.

swim_phases.name: Payment, issuance & in-force
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 10–15 · pay to ongoing management
swim_phases.note: Coverage attaches on payment; the policy is issued, dispatched, quality-checked, and set up for renewal alerts and payment monitoring.

swim_phases.name: Claims intake & closure
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 16–17 · lifecycle endpoints
swim_phases.note: Claims are registered and routed to the relevant claims SOP under settlement authority; closure handles expiry, cancellation and refund.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-013 against the Guidewire PolicyCenter lifecycle catalog
detail_slides: Steps 1–5 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter lifecycle catalog
Steps 6–9 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter lifecycle catalog
Steps 10–14 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter lifecycle catalog

items.name: Multi-channel application intake
items.detail: Portal, agent and paper intake with application ID and timestamp aligns with PolicyCenter submission intake.

items.name: Identity & document verification
items.detail: Identity documents are verified, but there is no explicit sanctions/PEP screening step or continuous monitoring, only a prerequisite note — a regulatory control gap.

items.name: Coverage eligibility check
items.detail: Coverage type, sum insured and period checked against product guidelines align with PolicyCenter eligibility.

items.name: Verification approval gate
items.detail: A supervisor approval gate before risk assessment aligns with PolicyCenter submission governance.

items.name: Risk assessment & rating
items.detail: A manual risk checklist plus rating engine is used; PolicyCenter recommends automated underwriting with a predictive risk model to widen straight-through processing.

items.name: Policy drafting & clause library
items.detail: Policies are drafted with Legal input for non-standard clauses; PolicyCenter generates documents from a configurable product/clause library, reducing manual drafting and error.

items.name: Terms review & approval gate
items.detail: Regulatory-compliance review with a terms-approval gate aligns with PolicyCenter terms governance.

items.name: Terms notification
items.detail: Formal multi-channel notification with a response deadline aligns with PolicyCenter offer communication.

items.name: Acceptance & e-signature
items.detail: Acceptance may be verbal; DocuSign exists but is not mandated. PolicyCenter requires an auditable e-signature acceptance of terms before issuance — a conduct and evidencing gap.

items.name: Payment & coverage attachment
items.detail: Coverage attaching only on confirmed payment aligns with PolicyCenter bind-on-payment control.

items.name: Issuance & document dispatch
items.detail: Issuance with policy number, effective date and 2-day document dispatch aligns with PolicyCenter issuance.

items.name: Record quality & data integrity
items.detail: Records are quality-checked manually within a day; PolicyCenter validates data integrity automatically as the system of record, reducing downstream errors.

items.name: Renewal & payment monitoring
items.detail: Automated renewal alerts and payment-schedule monitoring align with PolicyCenter lifecycle management.

items.name: Mid-term endorsements
items.detail: Mid-term changes are assessed manually with a 5-day endorsement SLA. PolicyCenter supports self-service and straight-through endorsements for standard changes — a service and efficiency gap.

items.name: Claims intake & routing
items.detail: Claim registration, ID issue and routing to the relevant claims SOP align with PolicyCenter-to-claims handoff.

items.name: Policy closure & refund
items.detail: Closure for expiry/cancellation/lapse with unearned-premium refund aligns with PolicyCenter cancellation.

critical_missing.title: KYC / sanctions screening & monitoring
critical_missing.detail: Identity documents are verified but there is no explicit sanctions/PEP screening step or continuous monitoring across the lifecycle — a regulatory control gap versus the catalog.

critical_missing.title: Mandatory e-signature acceptance
critical_missing.detail: Acceptance may be verbal and DocuSign is not mandated; the catalog requires an auditable e-signature acceptance of terms before issuance, a conduct and evidencing gap.

critical_missing.title: Automated underwriting & document generation
critical_missing.detail: Risk assessment and policy drafting are largely manual; the catalog uses automated underwriting with a predictive model and a configurable clause library to widen STP and cut error.

critical_missing.title: Self-service & straight-through endorsements
critical_missing.detail: Mid-term changes are assessed manually; the catalog supports self-service and straight-through endorsements for standard changes, improving service and efficiency.

control_bars.name: KYC / sanctions
control_bars.note: No explicit screening or monitoring step

remediations.title: Add KYC / sanctions screening & continuous monitoring
remediations.detail: Insert an explicit identity/sanctions screening step at application and continuous watchlist monitoring across the lifecycle; route hits to Compliance. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Mandate e-signature acceptance
remediations.detail: Require auditable DocuSign acceptance of terms before issuance, replacing verbal confirmation. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Introduce automated underwriting & clause-library generation
remediations.detail: Add automated underwriting with a predictive risk model and generate policy documents from a configurable product/clause library. Effort: 8 weeks.

remediations.title: Enable self-service & straight-through endorsements
remediations.detail: Allow standard mid-term changes to be self-served and processed straight-through, routing only complex endorsements to underwriting. Effort: 6 weeks.

remediations.title: Automate data-quality validation
remediations.detail: Validate policy data integrity automatically at issuance rather than manual QC, reducing downstream errors. Effort: 3 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Received via portal, agent or paper; application ID; timestamp
Personal details confirmed; identity documents verified
Identity proofed; sanctions/PEP screened; continuous monitoring enabled
Coverage type, sum insured and period checked against guidelines
Risk scored by predictive model; rules-clear cases straight-through; refer otherwise
Premium calculated on risk, coverage and rating factors; currency confirmed
Policy documents generated from a configurable product/clause library; Legal for exceptions
Wording reviewed for accuracy and regulatory compliance; final terms confirmed

opt_steps.activity: Notify policyholder of terms and price
opt_steps.output: Coverage, premium, schedule, start date and 10-day deadline notified
Auditable DocuSign acceptance of terms captured before issuance
Payment via card, banking, cheque or transfer; coverage attaches on confirmed payment
Policy generated in PAS; number and effective date assigned; document linked
Schedule, certificate and PDS by email within 2 days; hard copy if required
Policy data integrity validated automatically at issuance
Automated 90/60/30-day alerts; payment schedule monitored; missed payments flagged
Standard mid-term changes self-served and processed straight-through; complex routed to UW
Claim registered in CMS; routed to SOP-INS-011 or SOP-INS-012

opt_steps.activity: Close policy at end of term or on request

opt_swim_phases.note: v2.0 adds an explicit KYC/sanctions screening step and automated, model-scored underwriting before pricing.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Policy Lifecycle Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v2.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-013. Alignment to the Guidewire PolicyCenter lifecycle catalog increased from 67% to 90%. Critical additions: an explicit KYC/sanctions screening step with continuous monitoring, mandatory e-signature acceptance, automated underwriting with clause-library document generation, self-service straight-through endorsements, and automated data-quality validation.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the PolicyCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional automation or controls

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the end-to-end lifecycle process for administering an insurance policy from application submission to closure. Version 2.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Policy Lifecycle Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 90% alignment (up from 67% in v1.0).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to all personal and commercial lines policies across all channels, covering new business, mid-term amendments, renewals and closures.
Scope extended: KYC/sanctions screening, mandatory e-signature and self-service endorsements now apply across the policy lifecycle.
This procedure does NOT cover reinsurance treaty administration (SOP-RE-001), group member-level enrolment (SOP-INS-006), or claims settlement beyond intake (SOP-INS-011/012).

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Submits accurate application and documents; e-signs acceptance; pays premium; requests changes.
Registers and verifies applications; performs identity and KYC/sanctions screening; routes to underwriting.
Owns automated and manual underwriting; sets terms and pricing; approves within authority.
Generates and issues documents; maintains the system of record; processes endorsements and closure.
Processes premium payments; monitors schedules; handles refunds.
Registers and routes claims to the relevant claims SOP.

sections.title: 5. Screening & Acceptance Controls (New Section — v2.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the PolicyCenter catalog. KYC/sanctions screening and e-signature acceptance now gate the policy lifecycle.

sections.title: 6. Process Steps
blocks: Notify policyholder of terms and price
Registered in CMS; routed to claims SOP
Close policy at end of term or on request

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Application placed on hold; applicant contacted within 2 business days to clarify or resubmit.
Material misrepresentation: Underwriting and Compliance notified; application suspended pending review.
Maximum resolution period: 14 calendar days before withdrawal.
Agent/Policy Admin liaises with Underwriting on whether terms adjustment is possible within authority.
Maximum two revised offers; further negotiation escalated to Head of Underwriting.
No agreement within 10 business days: application closed as 'Not Taken Up'.
New exception. A sanctions/PEP match holds the application pending Compliance review before any bind.
Screening hit: application held; Compliance dispositions within 1 business day.
Confirmed match: application declined and reported per regulatory obligations.
Standard mid-term changes are self-served; complex changes assessed by Underwriting with premium adjustment.
Disputed Meridian-initiated cancellation: closure held 10 business days for internal review.
Regulatory cases escalated to Compliance and Legal; external dispute resolution advised if unresolved.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: System of record for the full policy lifecycle
Identity and watchlist screening and monitoring

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v2.0: KYC/sanctions, e-signature, automated underwriting, data-quality validation and self-service endorsements.
Screening gate: KYC/sanctions screened at application and monitored across the lifecycle.
E-signature: auditable acceptance of terms before issuance.
Automated underwriting: predictive scoring widens straight-through processing with governance.
Clause library: documents generated from approved, version-controlled templates.
Data integrity: policy data validated automatically at issuance.
Self-service endorsements: standard mid-term changes processed straight-through.
Immutable audit trail: application, screening, acceptance, issuance and change events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Initial release — full policy lifecycle SOP consolidating application, issuance, in-force management, claims intake, and closure

OPTIMISED: KYC/sanctions screening & monitoring; mandatory e-signature; automated underwriting & clause-library generation; self-service straight-through endorsements; automated data-quality validation; catalog alignment 67% → 90%
